Okay, what's up guys and girls? Being able to overcome obstacles and help people make a decision is one of the most valuable pieces of being in sales. It's it is persuasion, it's using influence, it's using humor, sarcasm, anything that you can to basically I mean to influence them to make a decision and really honestly you have to see this as help okay you have to see this as helping someone because most people are just afraid. The price is like reasonable to them which 500 bucks for a lot of people is for what they're getting from a value standpoint is worth it. So they're like okay I'll do it I'm in right now 50% so 50 people are gonna require some convincing alright and 20% of people are absolute assholes and will never sign up. So don't don't get upset about that okay because like you're gonna have people say no it's just part of the game alright it's part of the some people are dead broke some people like whatever it is like it just it or some people just don't value their health that much it's probably not the broke thing it's usually that they just don't value it that much and they usually are able to deal with anyway so but mind you the difference between 30% close rate and 80% close rate is just your ability to overcome obstacles and objections okay and this is where we're going to lead them down a thought path that will basically convince them that what they need to do is to sign up because this is like this is gonna be this is gonna be the vacation they've been wanting and we're gonna take them there alright so that's the reason for the obstacles and the three main categories of types of obstacles that are gonna come up are one of three things one is the stall close which is simply I need to think about it I don't make rash decisions I you know this is really interesting but let me sleep on it blah blah blah clear they need to take action today and sign up alright so without further ado the obstacle handling sequence
